title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.1 — Operational planning: change advisory board (CAB) approval evidence
exclusions: Does NOT approve or reject changes; attests that 'normal' and 'emergency' changes have the required CAB approval record before implementation.
evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_8_1_cab_approval' (hourly) emits per change: change_id, type, implemented_at, cab_approved_at, approval_met, approver, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 8.1 Operational planning and control — CAB approval adherence is the direct control signal.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Closing a change with approval_status='rejected' but state='implemented' returns approval_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A change approved by a CAB-role user before implementation is absent.

spl:
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state IN (implemented,closed) earliest=-7d
| stats latest(type) AS type latest(_time) AS implemented_at latest(approval_status) AS approval_status latest(approver) AS approver latest(approval_date) AS cab_approved_at BY change_id
| lookup cab_roles.csv approver OUTPUT is_authorised_cab
| eval approval_met=if(type="emergency",1,if(approval_status="approved" AND is_authorised_cab=1 AND cab_approved_at<=implemented_at,1,0))
| where approval_met=0
| table change_id type implemented_at cab_approved_at approver approval_met
description: Flags implemented changes that were not approved by an authorised CAB member prior to implementation. Clause 8.1 requires the organisation to plan, implement and control processes to meet information-security requirements — CAB approval is the direct control.
value: Turns the 'post-implementation review' into a pre-emptive signal. The Head of IT Operations sees the breach list within the hour.
implementation: (1) Export ServiceNow change events; (2) maintain cab_roles.csv listing authorised approvers; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) approval_met=0 opens a deficiency record with 7d remediation; (5) monthly Audit Committee report. Indexes required: `index=change`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=servicenow:change`, `sourcetype=servicenow:change;`.
visualization: Table of unapproved changes, bar chart of breach count by team, single value 'unapproved changes in last 7d'. Key fields: `approval_met`, `approval_status`, `approver`, `cab_approved_at`, `change_id`, `implemented_at`.
knownFalsePositives: Emergency changes legitimately bypass CAB; their follow-up retrospective approval must exist separately — this UC excludes type=emergency by design.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=change` sourcetype=servicenow:change; approver-role lookup `cab_roles.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export ServiceNow change events; (2) maintain cab_roles.csv listing authorised approvers; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) approval_met=0 opens a deficiency record with 7d remediation; (5) monthly Audit Committee report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state IN (implemented,closed) earliest=-7d
| stats latest(type) AS type latest(_time) AS implemented_at latest(approval_status) AS approval_status latest(approver) AS approver latest(approval_date) AS cab_approved_at BY change_id
| lookup cab_roles.csv approver OUTPUT is_authorised_cab
| eval approval_met=if(type="emergency",1,if(approval_status="approved" AND is_authorised_cab=1 AND cab_approved_at<=implemented_at,1,0))
| where approval_met=0
| table change_id type implemented_at cab_approved_at approver approval_met
```

Understanding this SPL

**ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.1 — Operational planning: change advisory board (CAB) approval evidence** — Turns the 'post-implementation review' into a pre-emptive signal. The Head of IT Operations sees the breach list within the hour.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=change` sourcetype=servicenow:change; approver-role lookup `cab_roles.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: change; **sourcetype**: servicenow:change. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=change, sourcetype=servicenow:change, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **change_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **approval_met** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where approval_met=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: approval_met, approval_status, approver, cab_approved_at, change_id, implemented_at, type.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of unapproved changes, bar chart of breach count by team, single value 'unapproved changes in last 7d'.

This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
